title: Blade Firmware Compliance per FRU (Cisco UCS B-Series and X-Series)
exclusions: UCS domains where the **authoritative** firmware state is delivered through **Cisco Intersight** (treat Intersight or cross-launch HCL as the source of truth, not this UCSM-only view); standalone **C-series CIMC** with no UCSM association; legacy **UCS Director**-only reports; and **vulnerability- or PSIRT-priority** compliance programs that need CVE and advisory joins — use a **Security**-pillar use case for that, not this operational baseline.
dataSources: `index=cisco_ucs` (or your org index) and `sourcetype=cisco:ucs:inventory` from the Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS. Each event that participates in the primary SPL carries `classId` (this query uses `firmwareRunning` for the running image on each child DN under a blade) and, in a `join` subsearch, `classId=computeBlade` for the **model** string. Rack C-series in UCSM are under `sys/rack-unit-…` and are out of this narrow blade scope.
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` ([Splunkbase 2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731), current release 4.3.1, Splunk-supported on 9.x/10.x and Splunk Cloud). Use the on-disk name with an **underscore**; some older downloads still show a **hyphen** in the file name only.
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-24h@h
| search classId="firmwareRunning"
| regex dn="^sys/chassis-[^/]+/blade-[^/]+/"
| eval fw_raw=trim(coalesce(version, ver, ""))
| where fw_raw!=""
| rex field=dn "^(?<server_mo>sys/chassis-\d+/blade-\d+)"
| rex field=dn "^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)/blade-(?<blade_slot>\d+)"
| eval ldn=lower(dn)
| eval fru_parent=if(match(dn,"/fw-"), replace(dn,"/fw-[^/]+$",""), dn)
| eval fru_category=case(
    like(ldn,"%/bios/%"),"BIOS",
    like(ldn,"%/board/boardctrl/%") OR like(ldn,"%/boardctrl/%"),"BoardController",
    (like(ldn,"%/adaptor-%") AND like(ldn,"%/mgmt/%")),"MezzAdapter",
    (like(ldn,"%/blade-%/mgmt%") OR like(ldn,"%/mgmt-ext%")) AND !like(ldn,"%/adaptor-%"),"CIMC",
    like(ldn,"%/storage-%") AND like(ldn,"%/disk-%"),"LocalDisk",
    (like(ldn,"%/storage-%") OR like(ldn,"%/mraid%") OR like(ldn,"%/sas%")) AND !like(ldn,"%/disk-%"),"StorageController",
    1=1,"OtherFRU"
  )
| join type=outer max=0 server_mo [
  search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-7d@d
  | search classId="computeBlade"
  | rex field=dn "^(?<server_mo>sys/chassis-\d+/blade-\d+)"
  | eval blade_model=trim(coalesce(model, mfgBoardType, ""))
  | stats latest(blade_model) as blade_model by server_mo
  ]
| fillnull value="Unknown" blade_model
| eval ver_norm=lower(replace(replace(fw_raw, " ", ""), "\(([^)]+)\)", ".\1"))
| stats values(ver_norm) as vn_list values(fw_raw) as fw_list max(_time) as last_seen
    by server_mo, fru_parent, fru_category, blade_model, chassis_id, blade_slot
| eval u=mvsort(vn_list)
| eval cnt=mvcount(u)
| eval low=if(cnt>0, mvindex(u,0), null())
| eval has_conflict=if(cnt>1 AND mvindex(u,0)!=mvindex(u,-1), 1, 0)
| lookup ucs_blade_firmware_baseline blade_model fru_category OUTPUT approved_version, package_name, notes
| eval app_n=if(isnotnull(approved_version) AND approved_version!="", lower(replace(replace(approved_version, " ", ""), "\(([^)]+)\)", ".\1")), null())
| eval compliance=case(
    blade_model="Unknown" OR isnull(approved_version) OR approved_version="", "NoBaseline",
    has_conflict=1, "MultipleVersionsActive",
    isnotnull(app_n) AND low=app_n, "Compliant",
    1=1, "Drifted"
  )
| eval running_display=if(cnt==1, mvindex(fw_list,0), mvjoin(fw_list, " | "))
| table last_seen, compliance, server_mo, chassis_id, blade_slot, blade_model, fru_category, fru_parent, running_display, low, app_n, approved_version, package_name, notes
| sort 0 compliance, server_mo, fru_category, fru_parent
description: Audits every blade-only `firmwareRunning` managed object in `cisco:ucs:inventory`, rolls it up per replaceable unit under each `sys/chassis-.../blade-...` anchor, and compares a normalised string to a **model × FRU category** lookup so you can see **which** FRU lags (BIOS, CIMC, mezz, SAS, NVMe) while rack servers and IOMs live in other UCs. Tie the fleet to **UC-19.1.3**; use this one when a change window needs **per-FRU** proof, not a single combined version per blade.
value: Half-right firmware is still wrong: the hypervisor and drivers care about the SAS pack even when the BIOS is already on the “gold” label. This search stops you from **aggregating to one “current firmware”** per blade that hides a drifting controller, which is the gap you hit when a bundle activation fails quietly on one FRU. For executives, it is a **governance and readiness** check before new clusters land on a chassis, not a full security vulnerability audit — the latter belongs to a different catalogue entry that consumes PSIRT and CVE data. Day-two teams use the table to split work between **network (mezz)**, **storage (SAS / NVMe)**, and **platform (BIOS, CIMC)**, and to attach evidence to the same change record when UC-19.1.3 already said the **domain** was in policy.
implementation: Enable the UCS inventory input in `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs`, deploy `ucs_blade_firmware_baseline.csv` and `transforms.conf` in a sidecar app, then save the `spl` as a report. Schedule a daily compliance review and, if you need paging, a second search filtered to `Drifted` and `MultipleVersionsActive` with throttle keys on `fru_parent`.
visualization: Sortable **Table** of `compliance`, `server_mo`, `blade_model`, `fru_category`, `fru_parent`, `running_display`, and `approved_version`; **Single value** of non-Compliant count; column **Bar chart** of top non-compliant `fru_category`; optional **Timechart** of daily `Drifted` counts during a bundle wave; a **drill-in** that filters the same report to a single `chassis_id` and `blade_slot` for war-room use.

references.title: Cisco UCS Manager Management Information Model, firmwareRunning class (XML API / Model)
Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS (Splunkbase 2731) — install and integration
Cisco UCS Manager Firmware Management Guide 4.2 (package and bundle naming reference)
Splunk — transforms.conf: configure CSV and external lookups

knownFalsePositives: • A blade in **decommissioned** or **removal** admin state can still return `firmwareRunning` rows for up to a full UCSM inventory day — looks like drift until the MO ages out. Cross-check with `computeBlade` power state before paging. • During BIOS/LCM, **two** `firmwareRunning` DNs (boot loader vs. running) can both be present; if **normalized** versions differ you get `MultipleVersionsActive` even though the window is healthy. • A **mezz VIC** often reports a **package stack** (loader + UEFI/option ROM) that reads like multiple “versions” for one install — compare only the lines your baseline names in `package_name`. • The UCSM string may show **4.2(3a)** while a spreadsheet was typed **4.2.3a**; the SPL normalises parentheses, but if one side is still a **marketing label** and the other is a long **build id**, you need both in `notes` until you align strings. • On **X210c / X410c** the TA may not expose the same I/O or GPU-adjacent MOs on **older 4.1.x TAs** as on 4.3.x, so a missing row is a **version gap** not proof of safety — upgrade the TA on the poller, then re-compare. • A permissive ad-hoc search that forgets `classId` can include **`firmwareUpdatable`** (candidate) instead of `firmwareRunning` (active) — the numbers look off until you lock the class. • UCSM refresh for inventory is on the order of **minutes**, not seconds; a just-activated bundle can still read old for **5–10 minutes** in Splunk. • An **emergency PSIRT** bundle was applied in the data hall before the **CSV owner** updated `ucs_blade_firmware_baseline.csv` — expect `Drifted` until the governance row catches up, even though risk accepted the new build.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` (Splunkbase 2731, 4.3.x) on the collection host and Search Heads. UCSM read-only user with inventory to `firmwareRunning` and `computeBlade`.
• Index `cisco_ucs` (or your org index); role `srchIndexesAllowed` must include it. Network: 443 to UCSM; fix TLS trust before tuning SPL. Time sync — compare `_time` to UCSM for first validation.
• Do not conflate with UC-19.1.3: that UC is the fleet firmware picture (IOM, racks, every class). This UC is per-blade, per-FRU for LCM cut-ins: BIOS, CIMC, mezz, SAS controller, NVMe, each on its own row.

Step 1 — Configure data collection, lookup files, and transforms
(1) Modular input (literal path and stanza): on the data-collection node, open `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/local/inputs.conf` (create `local` if missing). Do not place credentials in a chat; paste them only into Splunk’s credential object or a secrets vault your team already uses. A representative stanza (keys mirror what newer 4.3.x bundles ship; always diff against `default/inputs.conf` in the installed TA before you paste):

```ini
[cisco_ucs://dc01_prod_ucs_a]
disabled = 0
index = cisco_ucs
interval = 300
host = your_ucsm_fqdn_here
# Follow the add-on’s keys for: UCSM URL, credentials, organization scope, and
# the inventory class list. Never rename sourcetype away from cisco:ucs:inventory
# unless your Splunk admin also fixes props and field extractions in lockstep.
```

Run `splunk restart` (or the minimal restart the UI prompts) on the input host after the first good configuration. In Splunk Web: Settings → Data inputs → Cisco UCS (inventory) to confirm the input is green.

(2) Create your private app for lookups (literal path): use a name such as `ucs_firmware_ops` under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/ucs_firmware_ops/`. In that app, create the lookup file:

`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/ucs_firmware_ops/lookups/ucs_blade_firmware_baseline.csv`

Use UTF-8, Unix line endings, and a header that matches the SPL exactly:

```csv
blade_model,fru_category,approved_version,package_name,notes
B200 M5,BIOS,4.2(3a),UCS-MS-4-2-3-Infra,Gold baseline for 5108 B200 M5 — BIOS
B200 M5,BoardController,1.0(3d),BCTL-M4-B200-M5,Storage board controller
B200 M5,StorageController,25.4.0-0113,SAS-IR-4.2(3a),Cisco 12G SAS MRAID for M5
B200 M5,MezzAdapter,4.1(1d),UCSB-VIC-14XX-M5,1280/1440/1490 VIC on mezz
B200 M5,CIMC,4.2(1c),BMC-4-2(1c),BMC/CIMC for M5
B200 M6,BIOS,4.3(1b),UCS-MS-4-3-1,Gold BIOS for 5108 B200 M6
B200 M6,StorageController,16.16.1.-0016,SAS-IR-4-3(1a),Cisco M6 SAS controller
B200 M7,BIOS,4.3(2a),UCS-MS-4-3-2,Gold BIOS for 5108 B200 M7
X210c M6,BIOS,4.3(1b),UCS-MS-4-3-1,Gold for X210c in UCS X9508
X210c M6,MezzAdapter,4.2(1b),UCSB-VIC-1520,Front-port mezz
X210c M7,BIOS,4.3(2a),UCS-MS-4-3-2,Gold for X210c M7
X210c M7,CIMC,4.3(1c),BMC-4-3(1c),BMC in X210c
X410c M6,BIOS,4.3(1b),UCS-MS-4-3-1,Gold for X410c
```

Rows must reflect the exact strings UCSM shows after you validate a sample blade in the GUI. If a FRU is intentionally allowed to be absent (no mezz, no disk), it simply will not appear as `firmwareRunning`—that is not an error.

(3) transforms.conf (literal stanza) in the same app:

`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/ucs_firmware_ops/local/transforms.conf`

```ini
[ucs_blade_firmware_baseline]
filename = ucs_blade_firmware_baseline.csv
```

(4) Push the lookup app to every search peer that runs the join; on the SH test `| inputlookup ucs_blade_firmware_baseline | head 5`.

(5) Use `classId="firmwareRunning"` for running images and `classId="computeBlade"` for model; never rely on a vendor-TA-only tag that the Splunk add-on does not emit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the `spl` field from this use case. Then save as Report and Optional Alert (recommend: alert only on `compliance` in `Drifted` or `MultipleVersionsActive`, and treat `NoBaseline` as a governance follow-up, not a page in production).

Production SPL (same as `spl` field, duplicated here for copy-paste):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-24h@h
| search classId="firmwareRunning"
| regex dn="^sys/chassis-[^/]+/blade-[^/]+/"
| eval fw_raw=trim(coalesce(version, ver, ""))
| where fw_raw!=""
| rex field=dn "^(?<server_mo>sys/chassis-\d+/blade-\d+)"
| rex field=dn "^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)/blade-(?<blade_slot>\d+)"
| eval ldn=lower(dn)
| eval fru_parent=if(match(dn,"/fw-"), replace(dn,"/fw-[^/]+$",""), dn)
| eval fru_category=case(
    like(ldn,"%/bios/%"),"BIOS",
    like(ldn,"%/board/boardctrl/%") OR like(ldn,"%/boardctrl/%"),"BoardController",
    (like(ldn,"%/adaptor-%") AND like(ldn,"%/mgmt/%")),"MezzAdapter",
    (like(ldn,"%/blade-%/mgmt%") OR like(ldn,"%/mgmt-ext%")) AND !like(ldn,"%/adaptor-%"),"CIMC",
    like(ldn,"%/storage-%") AND like(ldn,"%/disk-%"),"LocalDisk",
    (like(ldn,"%/storage-%") OR like(ldn,"%/mraid%") OR like(ldn,"%/sas%")) AND !like(ldn,"%/disk-%"),"StorageController",
    1=1,"OtherFRU"
  )
| join type=outer max=0 server_mo [
  search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-7d@d
  | search classId="computeBlade"
  | rex field=dn "^(?<server_mo>sys/chassis-\d+/blade-\d+)"
  | eval blade_model=trim(coalesce(model, mfgBoardType, ""))
  | stats latest(blade_model) as blade_model by server_mo
  ]
| fillnull value="Unknown" blade_model
| eval ver_norm=lower(replace(replace(fw_raw, " ", ""), "\(([^)]+)\)", ".\1"))
| stats values(ver_norm) as vn_list values(fw_raw) as fw_list max(_time) as last_seen
    by server_mo, fru_parent, fru_category, blade_model, chassis_id, blade_slot
| eval u=mvsort(vn_list)
| eval cnt=mvcount(u)
| eval low=if(cnt>0, mvindex(u,0), null())
| eval has_conflict=if(cnt>1 AND mvindex(u,0)!=mvindex(u,-1), 1, 0)
| lookup ucs_blade_firmware_baseline blade_model fru_category OUTPUT approved_version, package_name, notes
| eval app_n=if(isnotnull(approved_version) AND approved_version!="", lower(replace(replace(approved_version, " ", ""), "\(([^)]+)\)", ".\1")), null())
| eval compliance=case(
    blade_model="Unknown" OR isnull(approved_version) OR approved_version="", "NoBaseline",
    has_conflict=1, "MultipleVersionsActive",
    isnotnull(app_n) AND low=app_n, "Compliant",
    1=1, "Drifted"
  )
| eval running_display=if(cnt==1, mvindex(fw_list,0), mvjoin(fw_list, " | "))
| table last_seen, compliance, server_mo, chassis_id, blade_slot, blade_model, fru_category, fru_parent, running_display, low, app_n, approved_version, package_name, notes
| sort 0 compliance, server_mo, fru_category, fru_parent
```

**Why each non-obvious clause exists (read before you “simplify” the search):**
• `classId="firmwareRunning"` — narrows the stream to the UCS MO that represents **active** firmware, not the repository catalog, not `firmwareUpdatable` candidate objects, and not unrelated classes.
• `regex dn=...` — keeps **blade-only** paths (`sys/chassis-.../blade-...`); that single guard removes **C-series** `rack-unit` DNs and most fabric/IOM DNs for free.
• `coalesce(version, ver, "")` — UCSM API attributes for “running” normally land in `version`. Some TAs and adapter rows expose a shorter `ver` key; **if your hand-written runbook from 2016 still said `running_fw`**, that key is not guaranteed on `firmwareRunning` rows on modern TA builds—bind your validation to `version` in UCSM, then make your lookup strings match the chosen canonical field, not a remembered alias.
• `fru_parent` — one hardware FRU (BIOS, mezz, disk, controller) can emit **more than one** `firmwareRunning` child (`fw-boot-loader` and `fw-running`); the parent path groups these so a mid-upgrade “two line” case becomes `MultipleVersionsActive` when **normalized** versions disagree, not a silent collapse into one “latest” value.
• `fru_category` from **DN and path shape** first — UCSM `type` on the MO is useful for confirmation, but **your automation must not depend** on a single token across every UCSM generation. The `case()` is aligned with the DN patterns Cisco documents for B-series; tweak only after you have five real DNs in `| stats count by dn` from your own domain.
• The **`join` to `computeBlade`** is how you get **blade model** in one query without inventing a vendor field on firmware rows that the TA does not emit. It is a bounded 7-day lookback; shorten or lengthen to match your poll cadence.
• **`ver_norm` parentheses flattening** — marketing bundles are written `4.2(3a)` while the API can show `4.2.3a` style strings. The same `replace()` is applied to both the running and approved sides so a cosmetic mismatch does not page at 03:00.
• The **`compliance` eval** is intentionally ordered: `NoBaseline` before drift so missing CSV governance does not look like a security incident; `MultipleVersionsActive` before `Compliant` so LCM in-flight states stay visible; `Compliant` only on exact normalized match.

**First-hour dashboard (under 60 minutes for a new Splunk user):** New Dashboard → add a **Table** from this saved report; set the time picker to **Last 24 hours**; set **20 minute auto-refresh**; pin one **Single value** of `count where compliance!="Compliant"`.

**Alert recipe:** add `| where compliance IN ("Drifted","MultipleVersionsActive")` and throttle 2–4 hours on `fru_parent` while UCSM re-polls.

Step 3 — Validate against Cisco UCS Manager
(1) Pick a **Drifted** row. Copy `server_mo` and the tail of `fru_parent` (everything after the blade anchor).
(2) In UCSM, navigate **Equipment** along `sys → chassis-N → blade-M` to match the left side of the DN. Open **Inventory → Firmware** (or the per-FRU tabs your UCSM build uses for BIOS, CIMC, Adapter, and Storage).
(3) Confirm the string you see in the GUI is one of the values in `running_display` on that FRU. If the GUI is shorter, expand the **show details** link—Cisco often abbreviates in the first column.
(4) If Splunk is correct and the **baseline** is behind an emergency one-off, update `ucs_blade_firmware_baseline.csv` with a reviewed change number in `notes`—this UC is about **governance and visibility**, not about declaring an emergency hotfix as “bad” forever.
(5) If UCSM and Splunk differ only briefly right after an activation, wait one inventory cycle (see Step 5) and re-check.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **RACI:** **Compute platform** owns the **CSV** and the UCSM LCM; **app owners** wait on the per-FRU table before new pools take traffic.
• **Pair with UC-19.1.3:** the fleet view answers how far the domain lags; this UC answers whether one blade is end-to-end ready for the next LCM cut-in.
• **Exports:** weekly CSV of non-Compliant rows to your existing evidence store; redact FQDNs for less-trusted shares.
• **Cloned panels:** split by `fru_category` (BIOS, Mezz, Storage, Disk) for team ownership.

Step 5 — Tuning and troubleshooting

• **Empty search while UC-19.1.3 works** — you likely still have inventory, but no `firmwareRunning` for blades in the lookback, or the TA version’s class name differs. Run `| stats count by classId` on raw inventory for a one-hour window, confirm `firmwareRunning` is present, and confirm your **regex** is not stricter than your site’s DNs.
• **All `NoBaseline`** — the lookup file is on the wrong SH, the CSV header has a typographical mismatch with `| lookup` field names, or the app containing `lookups/*.csv` is not visible to the running user. `| inputlookup ucs_blade_firmware_baseline` first.
• **`blade_model` is Unknown** — the `join` to `computeBlade` is failing (subsearch timeout, or wrong index). Widen the subsearch to `earliest=-30d@d` for a one-off audit, and verify `classId` on those rows in Step 1’s probe. Never fabricate a model string in SPL—fix the poll or RBAC.
• **Everything is `Compliant` but UCSM still shows a yellow LCM** — LCM and inventory refresh are **not** the same clock; check **pending activities** in UCSM and whether the FRU is waiting on power-cycle, not a poll gap.
• **Spikes of `OtherFRU`** — your `case()` list needs a new branch after you see three example DNs for a new card type; do not “solve” with a blanket `where fru_category!="OtherFRU"` until risk accepts blind spots.
• **Intersight-first domains where UCSM is secondary** — the firmware source of truth may be **not** the UCSM `firmwareRunning` table; that is a declared **exclusion**—treat the stream as out of scope, not a failed UC.
• **Rack C-series in the same index** — this search already excludes `rack-unit` by DN; if you need rack parity, use a different saved search—do not widen this UC to racks or you will lose the title’s promise of **per-blade** density.
• **Accidental `firmwareUpdatable` rows in an older, looser ad-hoc search** — the shipping SPL never references that class. If a copied query brings them in, you will see **candidate** not **running** version semantics—**keep `classId` locked** to `firmwareRunning` for this use case.
• **Performance complaints on 50k+ FRU events** — replace the `join` with a **prebuilt summary** `| outputlookup` from a 15-min scheduled search that only maps `server_mo` to `blade_model`, and join that file instead of a live `computeBlade` subsearch. That is the same outcome with lower search cost at scale.
• **UCSM GUI shows `4.2(3a)` and Splunk shows `4.2.3a` before normalization** — expected glyph variance; the **normalized** `low` and `app_n` fields are the ones the alert should compare. If you still get drift after both sides normalize, the strings are **actually** different builds—treat that as real.
• **A blade in admin-decommissioned state** — may still emit `firmwareRunning` rows for a time; pair with `operPower` on `computeBlade` or a decomm muting lookup.
grandmaExplanation: We line up the signed-off versions of every important chip and card inside each blade with what the domain actually has running, one layer at a time, so the people moving workloads during a change window can see the exact straggler and fix it in the right order without guessing from a single combined version string.
hardware: Cisco UCS B200 M5, B200 M6, B200 M7 blade servers in **UCS 5108** blade chassis; Cisco UCS **X210c M6**, **X210c M7**, and **X410c M6** compute nodes in the **UCS X9508** rack (X-series compute shelf). C-series **rack servers** and fabric extenders are out of this blade-only scope.
